changes.value:
builtin/skills/implement-loop/SKILL.md:26

The skill says: "Only call `ralph` with `op: "clear ralph"` when `kanban` `next task` returns no cards." However, there is no explicit loop-back instruction to re-query `next task` after each `/implement` call completes. The flow is: set ralph â†’ delegate to /implement â†’ delegate to /test â†’ ???. The skill does not state when or how to loop back to the `next task` check. A literal reading leaves the agent unsure whether to keep calling `/implement` or re-query kanban first.

Suggestion: Add an explicit loop step: after `/test` passes, call `kanban` `next task` again; if cards remain, continue; if none, clear ralph and summarise."
WARNING: implement-loop has no explicit termination check â€” could loop forever if /implement never clears cards
